2606.15753.title: RoboPIN: Grounded Embodied Reasoning via Pinned Chain-of-Thought
2606.15753.paper_summary: Embodied reasoning requires models to perceive task-relevant objects and spaces in physical environments and maintain consistent visual grounding throughout multi-step reasoning. However, current vision-language models rely on text-only or coordinate-augmented chain-of-thought, where entity references remain implicit and ambiguous. This may cause the reasoning process to decouple from visual evidence, entity references to drift across steps, and a causal disconnection between the reasoning trajectory and the final answer, with these problems further amplified in multi-view scenarios due to cross-view appearance changes. To address these issues, we propose Pinned Chain-of-Thought (\pincot{}), a structured reasoning paradigm that pins every reasoning step to visual evidence. \pincot{} introduces the concept of \reasoninganchor{}, which binds each task-relevant entity to a structured visual anchor with entity name, unique identity, view index, and spatial grounding, enabling consistent entity tracking across reasoning steps and views. We build a fully automated data generation pipeline to construct \dataset{}, a high-quality \pincot{}-formatted reasoning dataset. We then train \method{} through three-stage post-training that progressively injects embodied knowledge, structured reasoning ability, and process-supervised alignment, with rewards that directly constrain both anchor localization and identity consistency during reasoning. On 14 benchmarks covering embodied spatial reasoning, multi-view reasoning, and pointing, \method{} with only 4B parameters consistently outperforms 7B level open-source embodied models, achieving a 12\% average improvement over the strongest 7B baseline, Mimo-Embodied. Further analysis shows that \pincot{} improves grounding accuracy and cross-step identity consistency, validating the effectiveness of process supervision.
2606.15753.paper_summary_zh: 具身推理需要模型在物理環境中感知與任務相關的物體和空間，並在多步推理過程中保持一致的視覺基礎。<lb/>然而，目前的視覺-語言模型依賴於僅文字或坐標增強的思維鏈，其中實體參考保持隱含且模糊。<lb/>這可能導致推理過程與視覺證據脫節，實體參考在步驟之間漂移，以及推理軌跡與最終答案之間的因果斷裂，這些問題在多視角場景中因跨視角外觀變化而進一步加劇。<lb/>為了解決這些問題，我們提出了固定思維鏈（\pincot{}），這是一種結構化的推理範式，將每個推理步驟固定於視覺證據上。<lb/>\pincot{}引入了\reasoninganchor{}的概念，將每個與任務相關的實體綁定到一個結構化的視覺錨點，該錨點包含實體名稱、唯一身份、視圖索引和空間基礎，從而實現跨推理步驟和視圖的一致實體追蹤。<lb/>我們建立了一個完全自動化的數據生成管道，以構建\dataset{}，這是一個高質量的\pincot{}格式推理數據集。<lb/>然後，我們通過三階段的後訓練來訓練\method{}，該過程逐步注入具身知識、結構化推理能力和過程監督對齊，並提供直接約束錨點定位和身份一致性的獎勵。<lb/>在涵蓋具身空間推理、多視角推理和指向的14個基準上，僅有4B參數的\method{}始終超越7B級的開源具身模型，實現了對最強7B基準Mimo-Embodied的12\%平均改進。<lb/>進一步分析顯示，\pincot{}提高了基礎準確性和跨步驟身份一致性，驗證了過程監督的有效性。
2606.15753.author: Yaoting Huang et.al.
2606.15753.authors: Yaoting Huang, Yifu Yuan, Linqi Han, Chengwen Li, Shuoheng Zhang, Xianze Yao, Hongyao Tang, Yan Zheng, Jianye Hao
